Interprocessor Interrupt Event-handling Routines (tmc/ipi.h)
[Tilera Multicore Components (TMC&tm;) Library]

Detailed Description

Routines for handling IPI events.

Events generated by Interprocessor Interrupt (IPI), such as those generated by IO shims, can be handled at user level through the use of the support functions documented here. These functions will allow access to the IPI from user level, and allow the installation of a callback function to handle IPI events. The callback function will be called from user level without an OS context switch.

One handler may be installed for each IPI event on a particular CPU. Each CPU that handles an IPI event must activate the IPI functionality and install the handler for each event the CPU handles. Different CPUs may install different handlers for the same event.

By default, the IPI mechanism assumes every CPU in the current task's affinity set will be using the IPI mechanism. To provide a specific list of participating application CPUs, you must specify a cpumask that includes all the CPUs that will be participating. Either way, you must ensure that none of the CPUs that will be in the set are part of another IPI hardwall group. For a threaded application you may simply initialize with the desired cpumask, then bind the threads to run on particular CPUs before activing them. For a multi-process application, you should initialize in the parent process, then fork the children. See the <tmc/udn.h> header for more details of how to set up hardwalls on UDN, which is similar in spirit to setting up IPI groups.

When a CPU designates another CPU as a target to handle events from a shim, the target CPU must activate the IPI and install an event handler.

Installing an IPI event handler will result in the installation of a user-level interrupt handler. Only one user-level interrupt handler may be installed per interrupt, and that interrupt must be handled at user-level system-wide. Therefore if any CPU uses an IPI event handler, no CPU may install a different handler for the IPI interrupt.

Defines

#define TMC_IPI_NUM_EVENTS   32
 The number of IPI events.
#define TMC_IPI_MAX_EVENT   (TMC_IPI_NUM_EVENTS - 1)
 The maximum value for an IPI event.

Typedefs

typedef void(* tmc_ipi_func_t )(void *arg)
 tmc_ipi_func_t is the user-space C callback function.

Functions

int tmc_ipi_init (cpu_set_t *cpus)
 Initialize the IPI functionality for this process.
int tmc_ipi_activate (void)
 Activate the IPI functionality on this CPU.
int tmc_ipi_deactivate (void)
 Deactivate the IPI functionality on this CPU.
int tmc_ipi_event_install (int ipi, int event, tmc_ipi_func_t func, void *arg)
 Function to install a C callback entry for an IPI event in user space.
int tmc_ipi_event_alloc (int ipi, int event)
 Function to allocate a particular IPI event.
int tmc_ipi_event_alloc_first_available (int ipi)
 Function to allocate an IPI event, if one is available.
int tmc_ipi_event_dealloc (int ipi, int event)
 Function to deallocate a particular IPI event.

Define Documentation

#define TMC_IPI_NUM_EVENTS   32

The number of IPI events.

#define TMC_IPI_MAX_EVENT   (TMC_IPI_NUM_EVENTS - 1)

The maximum value for an IPI event.

Typedef Documentation

typedef void(* tmc_ipi_func_t)(void *arg)

tmc_ipi_func_t is the user-space C callback function.

Parameters:
arg Value specified at event registration time to pass to completion handler.

Function Documentation

int tmc_ipi_init ( cpu_set_t *  cpus  ) 

Initialize the IPI functionality for this process.

In a multi-threaded process, tmc_ipi_init() uses locking to ensure that two threads can not invoke it at the same time. In general, the initial parent thread should call tmc_ipi_init() before any other threads are created; if not, each thread should individually ensure that it calls tmc_ipi_init() prior to installing an IPI event handler. A thread should not assume that some other thread's call to tmc_ipi_init() happens early enough to allow the first thread to invoke tmc_ipi functions.

Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, 1 (setting errno to EINVAL) if already initialized properly, or zero.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_ipi_activate ( void   ) 

Activate the IPI functionality on this CPU.

This function must be called after tmc_ipi_init(), and before the event handler is installed by tmc_ipi_event_install().

Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, or zero.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_ipi_deactivate ( void   ) 

Deactivate the IPI functionality on this CPU.

Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, or zero.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c.

int tmc_ipi_event_install ( int  ipi,
int  event,
tmc_ipi_func_t  func,
void *  arg 
)

Function to install a C callback entry for an IPI event in user space.

The IPI capability must be initialized and activated before this function is called.

There are restrictions on the event handler function just as there are for any interrupt handler. For example, the handler may not hold a lock, and it may not call printf.

Parameters:
ipi IPI number to register. May specify any appropriate IPI number for BME applications; must be 0 for userspace programs running under Linux.
event IPI event within IPI number to register. Must be in the range 0 to TMC_IPI_MAX_EVENT.
func User-space C callback function to bind to the interrupt.
arg Value to be passed to the completion handler at event-handling time.
Returns:
0 on success, -1 on failure (and sets errno), 1 if this function has been called before and an event handler is already installed.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_ipi_event_alloc ( int  ipi,
int  event 
)

Function to allocate a particular IPI event.

Parameters:
ipi IPI number to register. May specify any appropriate IPI number for BME applications; must be 0 for userspace programs running under Linux.
event IPI event within IPI number to try to allocate. Must be in the range 0 to TMC_IPI_MAX_EVENT.
Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, allocated event number on success.

int tmc_ipi_event_alloc_first_available ( int  ipi  ) 

Function to allocate an IPI event, if one is available.

Parameters:
ipi IPI number to register. May specify any appropriate IPI number for BME applications; must be 0 for userspace programs running under Linux.
Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, allocated event number on success.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_ipi_event_dealloc ( int  ipi,
int  event 
)

Function to deallocate a particular IPI event.

Parameters:
ipi IPI number to register. May specify any appropriate IPI number for BME applications; must be 0 for userspace programs running under Linux.
event IPI event within IPI number to deallocate. Must be in the range 0 to TMC_IPI_MAX_EVENT. Event must have previously been allocated.
Returns:
-1 (setting errno) on error, 0 on success.
